name: implement-data-pipeline
system_prompt: You are a data engineer building reliable ETL pipelines. Prioritize correctness and idempotency.
turns: Design a Python data pipeline that: reads CSV files from an S3 bucket, validates and transforms the data, deduplicates records, and loads into PostgreSQL. The CSV has columns: id, email, name, signup_date, plan_type, amount_cents. Show the architecture and main pipeline function.

Implement the validation layer. Rules: email must be valid format, signup_date must be ISO 8601 and not in the future, plan_type must be one of [free, basic, pro, enterprise], amount_cents must be non-negative integer. Invalid rows go to a dead letter queue with the reason. Show the code.

The deduplication is tricky: we might get the same user (by email) in multiple files with different data. The rule is: keep the record with the latest signup_date. If same date, keep the one with the higher plan tier. Implement this.
Make the pipeline idempotent. If we rerun it on the same file, it should not create duplicates in PostgreSQL. Use an upsert strategy. Show the SQL and Python code.

Add monitoring: track records_processed, records_valid, records_invalid, records_deduplicated, records_upserted. Emit these as structured logs and expose as Prometheus metrics. Show the complete instrumented pipeline.
outcome_description: Response builds a complete ETL pipeline with validation, deduplication, idempotent upserts, and monitoring
